prescribing_info.title: LEXAPRO (escitalopram) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
LEXAPRO (ESCITALOPRAM OXALATE) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Allergan/AbbVie

BLACK BOX WARNING:
SUICIDALITY AND ANTIDEPRESSANT DRUGS - Antidepressants increased the risk of suicidal thinking and behavior in children, adolescents, and young adults in short-term studies. Monitor closely for clinical worsening and emergence of suicidal thoughts and behaviors. Not approved for use in pediatric patients less than 12 years of age.

MECHANISM: Selective serotonin reuptake inhibitor (SSRI)

INDICATIONS:
- Major Depressive Disorder (adults and adolescents 12-17)
- Generalized Anxiety Disorder (adults)

DOSAGE - MAJOR DEPRESSIVE DISORDER:

Adults:
- Initial: 10 mg once daily
- May increase to 20 mg once daily after minimum 1 week
- MAXIMUM: 20 mg/day

Adolescents (12-17 years):
- Initial: 10 mg once daily
- May increase to 20 mg after minimum 3 weeks
- MAXIMUM: 20 mg/day

DOSAGE - GENERALIZED ANXIETY DISORDER:

Adults:
- Initial: 10 mg once daily
- May increase to 20 mg after minimum 1 week if needed
- MAXIMUM: 20 mg/day

ELDERLY DOSING:
- Recommended: 10 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 10 mg/day in patients >65 years

HEPATIC IMPAIRMENT:
- 10 mg once daily
- MAXIMUM: 10 mg/day

RENAL IMPAIRMENT:
- Mild to moderate: No adjustment
- Severe (CrCl <20 mL/min): Use with caution

CYP2C19 POOR METABOLIZERS:
- Recommended: 10 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 10 mg/day

ADMINISTRATION:
- Once daily, morning or evening
- With or without food
- Oral solution: 10 mg = 5 mL

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Hypersensitivity to escitalopram or citalopram
- Concomitant use of MAOIs or within 14 days of stopping MAOI
- Concomitant use with pimozide
- Initiation in patients receiving linezolid or IV methylene blue

SEROTONIN SYNDROME WARNING:
- Potentially life-threatening
- Risk with MAOIs, triptans, tramadol, tryptophan, St. John's wort, other serotonergic drugs
- Symptoms: Agitation, hallucinations, tachycardia, hyperthermia, hyperreflexia, incoordination
- Discontinue and provide supportive care

QT PROLONGATION:
- Dose-dependent QTc prolongation
- Avoid in patients with congenital long QT syndrome
- Use with caution with other QT-prolonging drugs
- Maximum dose 10 mg in patients with risk factors

WARNINGS:
- Activation of mania/hypomania
- Seizures
- Abnormal bleeding (especially with NSAIDs, aspirin, warfarin)
- Hyponatremia (SIADH)
- Sexual dysfunction
- Angle-closure glaucoma

DISCONTINUATION SYNDROME:
- Taper gradually when discontinuing
- Symptoms: Dysphoric mood, irritability, agitation, dizziness, paresthesias, anxiety, confusion
- Reduce dose gradually over several weeks

DRUG INTERACTIONS:
- MAOIs: Contraindicated
- Pimozide: Contraindicated
- CYP2C19 inhibitors: May increase levels
- Serotonergic drugs: Serotonin syndrome risk
- Drugs affecting hemostasis: Bleeding risk
- CNS drugs: Use caution

ADVERSE REACTIONS (≥5%):
- Nausea (18%)
- Headache (24%)
- Somnolence (9%)
- Insomnia (12%)
- Ejaculation disorder (14%)
- Diarrhea (8%)
- Dry mouth (9%)
- Fatigue (5%)

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 5 mg, 10 mg, 20 mg tablets
- 5 mg/5 mL oral solution

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 021323
Label Revision: 01/2024
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=13bb8267-1cab-43e5-acae-55a4d957630a

prescribing_info.title: ABILIFY (aripiprazole) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
ABILIFY (ARIPIPRAZOLE) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Otsuka/Bristol-Myers Squibb

BLACK BOX WARNINGS:

1. ELDERLY WITH DEMENTIA-RELATED PSYCHOSIS: Increased risk of death. Not approved for dementia-related psychosis.

2. SUICIDALITY IN PEDIATRIC AND YOUNG ADULT PATIENTS: Increased risk of suicidal thinking and behavior in pediatric and young adult patients with major depressive disorder and other psychiatric disorders.

MECHANISM: Atypical antipsychotic; partial agonist at D2 and 5-HT1A receptors, antagonist at 5-HT2A receptors

INDICATIONS:
- Schizophrenia (adults and adolescents 13-17)
- Bipolar I disorder (acute manic/mixed; maintenance; adjunct to lithium/valproate)
- Major depressive disorder (adjunct)
- Irritability with autistic disorder (6-17 years)
- Tourette's disorder (6-18 years)

DOSAGE - SCHIZOPHRENIA:

Adults:
- Initial and target: 10-15 mg once daily
- May increase to maximum after 2 weeks if needed
- MAXIMUM: 30 mg/day

Adolescents (13-17):
- Initial: 2 mg/day
- Titrate to 5 mg after 2 days, then to 10 mg after 2 more days
- Target: 10 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 30 mg/day

DOSAGE - BIPOLAR I (Manic/Mixed):

Adults:
- Initial: 15 mg once daily (monotherapy)
- Initial: 10-15 mg once daily (with lithium/valproate)
- MAXIMUM: 30 mg/day

Pediatric (10-17):
- Initial: 2 mg/day
- Target: 10 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 30 mg/day

DOSAGE - MAJOR DEPRESSION (Adjunct):
- Initial: 2-5 mg once daily
- Titrate at intervals of no less than 1 week
- Dose range: 2-15 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 15 mg/day

DOSAGE - AUTISTIC DISORDER IRRITABILITY (6-17):
- Initial: 2 mg/day
- Target: 5-10 mg/day
- Increase no more than 5 mg/week
- MAXIMUM: 15 mg/day

DOSAGE - TOURETTE'S (6-18):
- <50 kg: Initial 2 mg/day, target 5 mg/day, max 10 mg/day
- ≥50 kg: Initial 2 mg/day, target 10 mg/day, max 20 mg/day

CYP2D6 POOR METABOLIZERS:
- Administer 50% of usual dose
- Reduce if on CYP3A4 or CYP2D6 inhibitors

CONCOMITANT CYP INTERACTIONS:
- Strong CYP3A4 inhibitor (ketoconazole): Reduce aripiprazole by 50%
- Strong CYP2D6 inhibitor (fluoxetine, paroxetine): Reduce by 50%
- Strong CYP3A4 inducer (carbamazepine): Double aripiprazole dose

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Hypersensitivity to aripiprazole

WARNINGS:
- Cerebrovascular adverse events in elderly with dementia
- Neuroleptic malignant syndrome
- Tardive dyskinesia
- Metabolic changes (hyperglycemia, dyslipidemia, weight gain)
- Orthostatic hypotension
- Leukopenia/neutropenia
- Seizures
- Cognitive and motor impairment
- Body temperature dysregulation
- Dysphagia
- Pathological gambling and compulsive behaviors

METABOLIC MONITORING:
- Baseline: Weight, fasting glucose, lipids
- Weight: Monthly for 3 months, then quarterly
- Glucose/lipids: 12 weeks after initiation, then annually

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Akathisia (10-25%)
- Sedation/somnolence (8-16%)
- Tremor (6%)
- Headache
- Weight gain (less than other atypicals)
- Nausea/vomiting
- Insomnia

LONG-ACTING INJECTABLE (Abilify Maintena):
- 400 mg IM monthly
- 300 mg IM monthly with certain CYP inhibitors
- Administer in deltoid or gluteal
- First injection: Continue oral aripiprazole for 14 days

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 2, 5, 10, 15, 20, 30 mg tablets
- 10, 15 mg orally disintegrating tablets
- 1 mg/mL oral solution
- 9.75 mg/1.3 mL IM injection (acute agitation)
- 300, 400 mg IM extended-release (Maintena)

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 021436
Label Revision: 11/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=c040bd1d-45b7-49f2-93ea-aed7220b30ac

prescribing_info.title: XANAX (alprazolam) Prescribing Information
prescribing_info.content:
XANAX (ALPRAZOLAM) - FULL PRESCRIBING INFORMATION

MANUFACTURER: Pfizer

BLACK BOX WARNING:
OPIOID COMBINATION RISKS - Concomitant use of benzodiazepines and opioids may result in profound sedation, respiratory depression, coma, and death. Reserve concomitant prescribing for patients for whom alternative treatment options are inadequate. Limit dosages and durations. Monitor for respiratory depression and sedation.

ABUSE, MISUSE, AND ADDICTION WARNING:
- Alprazolam is a Schedule IV controlled substance
- Risk of abuse, misuse, and addiction which can lead to overdose and death
- Use only as prescribed
- Risk increases with concomitant CNS depressants

MECHANISM: Benzodiazepine; binds to GABA-A receptors enhancing GABAergic inhibition

INDICATIONS:
- Anxiety disorders
- Panic disorder with or without agoraphobia

DOSAGE - ANXIETY:
- Initial: 0.25-0.5 mg three times daily
- Titrate: Increase at intervals of 3-4 days
- Target: Lowest effective dose
- MAXIMUM: 4 mg/day (in divided doses)

DOSAGE - PANIC DISORDER:

Immediate Release:
- Initial: 0.5 mg three times daily
- Increase: No more than 1 mg/day every 3-4 days
- Target: 1-10 mg/day
- Mean effective dose: 5-6 mg/day
- MAXIMUM: 10 mg/day

Extended Release (XR):
- Initial: 0.5-1 mg once daily
- Titrate: Increase no faster than 1 mg every 3-4 days
- Target: 3-6 mg once daily
- MAXIMUM: 10 mg/day

ELDERLY OR DEBILITATED:
- Initial: 0.25 mg two or three times daily
- Increase gradually if needed
- Lower doses generally effective

HEPATIC IMPAIRMENT:
- Advanced cirrhosis: Initial 0.25 mg two or three times daily
- Titrate cautiously

DISCONTINUATION:
- Taper gradually - do NOT stop abruptly
- Reduce dose by no more than 0.5 mg every 3 days
- Some patients may need even slower taper
- Withdrawal symptoms: Seizures, tremor, abdominal/muscle cramps, vomiting, sweating, anxiety

CONTRAINDICATIONS:
- Hypersensitivity to benzodiazepines
- Concomitant ketoconazole or itraconazole
- Acute narrow-angle glaucoma

WARNINGS:
- Dependence and withdrawal reactions
- Risk of abuse and misuse
- CNS depression (especially with opioids)
- Rebound and withdrawal anxiety
- Risk of fetal harm (neonatal sedation, withdrawal)
- Mania in patients with depression

DRUG INTERACTIONS:
- CYP3A inhibitors: Increased alprazolam levels
  - Strong inhibitors (ketoconazole, itraconazole): Contraindicated
  - Moderate inhibitors: Use caution, consider dose reduction
- Opioids: Profound sedation, respiratory depression
- CNS depressants: Additive effects
- Alcohol: Potentiates effects

ADVERSE REACTIONS:
- Drowsiness/sedation (41%)
- Light-headedness (21%)
- Depression (13%)
- Headache (12%)
- Confusion (10%)
- Insomnia (9%)
- Dry mouth (15%)
- Constipation (10%)
- Diarrhea (10%)
- Cognitive impairment
- Coordination problems

OVERDOSE:
- Symptoms: Somnolence, confusion, impaired coordination, diminished reflexes, coma
- Treatment: Supportive care, flumazenil (caution: may precipitate seizures in dependent patients)

DEPENDENCE:
- Physical dependence develops with prolonged use
- Risk factors: High dose, long duration, history of substance abuse
- Psychological dependence also possible
- Abrupt discontinuation can be life-threatening

HOW SUPPLIED:
- 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2 mg tablets
- XR: 0.5, 1, 2, 3 mg extended-release tablets
- 1 mg/mL oral solution
- Orally disintegrating tablets: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 2 mg

SOURCE PROVENANCE:
This document summarizes key information from the FDA-approved prescribing information.
FDA Application: NDA 018276
Label Revision: 09/2023
For complete prescribing information, refer to the official FDA label at: https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=afc39f97-c7a4-4c3f-81df-5cd5aa66ac1c
